LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SATINDER SINGH'S MOTION IN SUPPORT OF PETITION FOR ORDER RELIEVING PETITION FROM GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 945.4 IS GRANTED. (CCP 946.6(C)(1).) PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT THE FAILURE TO PRESENT A TIMELY CLAIM UNDER GOV'T CODE 945.4 WAS THE RESULT OF SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. PLAINTIFF WAS UNAWARE OF CCSF'S INVOLVEMENT IN THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT UNTIL 7/03/13 WHEN DEFENDANT REED'S INSURER, YORK RISK SERVICES, INFORMED HIM THAT MR. REED MAY HAVE BEEN DRIVING THE VEHICLE DURING THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF HIS EMPLOYMENT WITH CCSF. ONCE INFORMED, PLAINTIFF ACTED DILIGENTLY, AND CCSF HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY DEMONSTRATED ANY PREJUDICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAROLINA MICHEL'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO CONSOLIDATE CASES PURSUANT TO CCP 1048(A) (CGC-14-542359 WITH CASE CGC-14-536941) IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT CAROLINA MICHEL'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE IS GRANTED FOR TRIAL AND CASE MANAGEMENT PURPOSES ONLY. "THERE ARE TWO TYPES OF CONSOLIDATION: A COMPLETE CONSOLIDATION RESULTING IN A SINGLE ACTION, AND A CONSOLIDATION OF SEPARATE ACTIONS FOR TRIAL. UNDER THE FORMER PROCEDURE, WHICH MAY BE UTILIZED WHERE THE PARTIES ARE IDENTICAL AND THE CAUSES COULD HAVE BEEN JOINED, THE PLEADINGS ARE REGARDED AS MERGED, ONE SET OF FINDINGS IS MADE, AND ONE JUDGMENT IS RENDERED. IN A CONSOLIDATION FOR TRIAL, THE PLEADINGS, VERDICTS, FINDINGS AND JUDGMENTS ARE KEPT SEPARATE; THE ACTIONS ARE SIMPLY TRIED TOGETHER FOR THE SAKE OF CONVENIENCE AND JUDICIAL ECONOMY." (SANCHEZ V. SUPERIOR COURT (1988) 203 CAL.APP.3D 1391, 1396.) CONSOLIDATION OF AN ACTION IS PERMISSIVE, AND IT IS FOR THE TRIAL COURT TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE CONSOLIDATION IS FOR ALL PURPOSES OR FOR TRIAL ONLY. ?(SEE HAMILTON V. ASBESTOS CORP., LTD. (2000) 95 CAL.RPTR.2D 701.) CONSOLIDATION FOR TRIAL PURPOSES IS APPROPRIATE HERE BECAUSE THERE ARE TWO DIFFERENT PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO FILED A CROSS-COMPLAINT NAMING LUXOR CABS, INC. IN ONLY ONE OF THE ACTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Malcolm Barrack of Tarkington O'Neil, Tel. No. 1-707-576-0121 appearing
for the Defendant;
Atty. Ghaza Sharif, Tel. No. 1-510-465-3922 appearing for the Defendant Minjigdorj Erdenekhuu by telephone appearance.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Balwant Gill's motion for good faith settlement is granted. The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Gill has little, if any, liability for the accident. (Barrack Dec., Ex. A, Snook Depo. 44:19-47:10.) The most compelling evidence-the video-shows that plaintiff Satinder Singh's vehicle was pushed into the lane where Mr. Gill was driving, Mr. Gill moved to the shoulder to avoid Mr. Singh's vehicle, and Mr. Gill made slight contact with Mr. Singh's vehicle. (Barrack Dec., Ex. B.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that Mr. Gill has no liability. The settlement is therefore in the "ballpark" even though Mr. Singh's claimed damages are currently unsettled. There is substantial evidence that the settlement was made in good faith. "[P]ractical considerations obviously require that the evaluation be made on the basis of information available at the time of settlement." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Order signed in open court. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-542359 WITH CASE CGC-14-536941
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bradley Bush of Bradley & Gmelich telephone number 818-243-5200 appeared by telephone for Defendants SF Town Taxi, Inc. and Khalid Kahn.
Daniel Balich of Kern, Noda, Devine & Segal telephone number 415-474-1900 for Defendant Carolina Michel.

Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAROLINA MICHEL'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO CONSOLIDATE CASES PURSUANT TO CCP 1048(a) (CGC-14-542359 WITH CASE CGC-14-536941) is granted in part. Defendant Carolina Michel's motion to consolidate is granted for trial and case management purposes only. "There are two types of consolidation: a complete consolidation resulting in a single action, and a consolidation of separate actions for trial. Under the former procedure, which may be utilized where the parties are identical and the causes could have been joined, the pleadings are regarded as merged, one set of findings is made, and one judgment is rendered. In a consolidation for trial, the pleadings, verdicts, findings and judgments are kept separate; the actions are simply tried together for the sake of convenience and judicial economy." (Sanchez v. Superior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396.) Consolidation of an action is permissive, and it is for the trial court to determine whether the consolidation is for all purposes or for trial only. ?(See Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp., Ltd. (2000) 95 Cal.Rptr.2d 701.) Consolidation for trial purposes is appropriate here because there are two different plaintiffs and Defendant City and County of San Francisco filed a cross-complaint naming Luxor Cabs, Inc. in only one of the actions. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
